page.plus.title: Persistent Cart for Shopify Plus — Cross-Device Carts
page.plus.desc: Persistent Cart syncs signed-in shoppers' carts across devices and browsers on Shopify Plus — high-volume ready, Built for Shopify, and trusted since 2016.
page.plus.h1: Cross-device cart persistence built for Shopify Plus
page.plus.lede: Persistent Cart gives Shopify Plus stores cross-device cart sync for signed-in shoppers: a logged-in customer's cart is saved on every change and restored on any device or browser. It's Built for Shopify, has run on high-volume, high-AOV stores since 2016, and on Plus it's the $99.99 plan with every feature included.
page.plus.s1.h: Why does Shopify Plus need a persistent cart app?
page.plus.s1.b:
Because Shopify doesn't sync carts across devices natively — not even on Plus. A default Shopify cart lives in a browser cookie that lasts about two weeks, tied to one browser on one device, not to the customer's account. A shopper who adds items on mobile and returns on a laptop finds an empty cart.

On a high-volume, high-AOV Plus store, that gap is expensive: more sessions, larger carts, and more device-switching between research and purchase. Persistent Cart closes it for signed-in shoppers — saving the cart on every change and restoring it on any device or browser they log in from — so the carts your traffic builds don't quietly reset.
page.plus.s2.b:
Yes. Persistent Cart has run on Shopify stores since 2016 — roughly a ten-year track record — including hundreds of Shopify Plus stores and thousands of merchants overall. It's Built for Shopify, Shopify's bar for apps that meet their highest standards for quality, performance, and merchant experience.

The app is a focused specialist: it does cart persistence and does it reliably, rather than bundling a dozen loosely related features. It runs through a theme app extension on storefront page load, saving the cart on every add, update, or change. For high-volume stores, that narrow, well-worn scope is the point — fewer moving parts, a decade of real-world use, and a 4.8 star App Store rating behind it.
page.plus.s3.h: Does it hold up under high volume and large carts?
page.plus.s3.b:
Yes. Persistent Cart saves each signed-in customer's cart keyed to their customer ID and your store domain, independently of every other shopper, so concurrent traffic doesn't pool or collide. The save fires on each cart mutation and the restore happens on login, so the work scales with individual shoppers rather than depending on any single shared bottleneck.

It also handles large, complex carts — long multi-line orders with quantities, notes, and cart attributes preserved. For Plus stores running flash sales, large catalogs, or B2B volumes, the cart a shopper rebuilds on a second device matches the one they left, item for item.
page.plus.s4.h: What does Persistent Cart cost on Shopify Plus?
page.plus.s4.b:
On Shopify Plus, Persistent Cart is the $99.99-per-month plan — and every feature is included. Pricing is matched to your Shopify subscription plan rather than gated by features or usage, so Plus stores get the complete app, the same one smaller stores run, with nothing held back on a higher tier.

Paid plans come with a 30-day trial, and there's a Free Starter plan for testing. For a high-AOV Plus store, the maths is straightforward: the plan is a fixed monthly cost, while a single recovered high-value cart can cover it many times over. Charges are billed through Shopify, so it sits inside your existing Shopify billing.
page.plus.s5.h: How do Plus stores install and run it?
page.plus.s5.b:
Setup is one click and one switch. Install Persistent Cart from the Shopify App Store, then enable the app embed in your theme editor — there's no theme-code editing and nothing to maintain in your layout files. It runs through a theme app extension, so it sits cleanly alongside the rest of your Plus stack.

Day to day it's quiet by design. The default on login is a silent additive merge: the shopper's saved cart restores with no popup, email, or opt-in. There's an optional prompt to let shoppers keep or combine carts if you prefer, but most Plus stores leave the default on so returning shoppers simply pick up where they left off.
page.plus.faq.q1.q: Is Persistent Cart Built for Shopify?
page.plus.faq.q1.a: Yes. Persistent Cart carries the Built for Shopify designation, Shopify's mark for apps that meet their highest standards for quality, performance, and merchant experience. It's also one of the longest-running cart apps in the ecosystem, live since 2016, with a 4.8 star App Store rating and hundreds of Shopify Plus stores among its installs.
page.plus.faq.q2.q: How much is Persistent Cart for a Shopify Plus store?
page.plus.faq.q2.a: On Shopify Plus it's $99.99 per month, with every feature included. Pricing is matched to your Shopify plan rather than to features or usage, so nothing is held back on the Plus tier — you get the same complete app smaller stores run. Paid plans include a 30-day trial, and billing goes through Shopify alongside your other charges.
page.plus.faq.q3.q: Will it slow down a high-traffic Plus storefront?
page.plus.faq.q3.a: It's built to stay light. Persistent Cart loads through a theme app extension on page load and saves each cart on change, with work scoped per signed-in shopper rather than through a shared bottleneck. It's been refined on high-volume stores since 2016 and meets the Built for Shopify performance bar. As with any app, test on your theme, but it's designed to run quietly at scale.
